signals.why_it_matters: Proxy for delivered-cost pressure in trucking and refrigerated distribution.

notes: Yahoo Finance futures endpoint returned 429 during build, so FRED is the durable no-key public data layer.
X/social context is stored in data/x_context.md from X Search summaries.

brief.headline: Restaurant Margin Snapshot: Pricing Power Intact as Soy Softens; Diesel Divergence Flags Freight Risk
brief.abstract: Otto Margin Desk synthesizes degraded but directional signals: Food-away-from-home CPI remains elevated (3.5%-3.7% context), supporting restaurant pricing power, while soybean PPI shows downward pressure on favorable U.S. weather. Diesel retail displays a divergence between FRED ($4.832/gal) and X/social ($5.00-$5.21), elevating freight-cost uncertainty. All FRED series fell back to single-point seeds due to timeouts, and Yahoo Finance futures returned 429, limiting quantitative edge. Confidence is medium-low. Immediate plays: lock protein/soy-linked contracts, audit fuel surcharges, and maintain menu pricing discipline.

signals.name: U.S. No. 2 Diesel Retail Price
signals.movement: Snapshot single-point; X context flags $5.00-$5.21 range, implying upward volatility or regional dispersion
signals.implication: Reefer/dry-van delivered-cost risk is elevated; fuel surcharge leakage likely

signals.name: PPI: Soybeans
signals.movement: Downward pressure per X/social (favorable U.S. growing weather)
signals.implication: Protein and feed input costs easing; window to lock contracts before export demand firms

signals.name: CPI: Food
signals.movement: Insufficient time-series history; broad inflation persistence implied by context
signals.implication: Grocery cost base remains firm, limiting at-home substitution tailwinds

signals.name: CPI: Food Away From Home
signals.implication: Operator pricing power and margin pass-through environment remain supportive

operator_play: Negotiate protein and soymeal-linked supply contracts immediately while PPI shows softening and weather remains favorable.
Audit freight invoices and carrier fuel-surcharge schedules; diesel dispersion ($4.83 FRED vs $5.00+ X) signals volatility and potential leakage.
Maintain menu pricing discipline; the food-away-from-home CPI premium supports pass-through without significant demand destruction.
If operating private fleet, consider 60-90 day diesel hedges or surcharge caps to lock reefer costs.
risk_flags: All FRED series returned timeout fallback to single-point seeds; month-over-month and year-over-year trends are unavailable.
Diesel price divergence between FRED snapshot ($4.832/gal) and X/social ($5.00-$5.21/gal) introduces material freight-cost uncertainty.
Soybean PPI is stale (May) and weather-driven softness can reverse on export demand or forecast shifts.
Yahoo Finance futures endpoint returned 429, removing real-time commodity futures edge from this briefing.

payment.reason: No Stripe test secret in runtime; using signed local checkout.session.completed event.
